item.description:
## 一、被忽略的"叙事补全机制"：市场不是被收益率打跌的，而是被自己的强势打跌的
视频里有一段话值得反复咀嚼——**"过去一个多月市场太强了，硬件太强了，芯片太强了。所以一旦回调，人们立刻去找原因，恰好赶上收益率上行，逻辑就被绑在一起了。"**
这其实揭示了一个让人意外的**因果倒置现象**：财经媒体并不是在解释市场，而是在为市场的情绪波动**事后配字幕**。30年期美债收益率在2023年下半年就突破过5%，但那并没有终结牛市。所以"收益率破5% = 熊市"并不是规律，而是一种**叙事工件（narrative artifact）**——它在市场需要解释时才会被激活。
跨领域连接：这和**神经科学里的"裂脑实验"**惊人地相似。当大脑某半球做出一个决定后，另一半球（语言中枢）会立刻编造一个看似合理的理由。市场的"日报评论体系"就是金融世界的语言中枢——价格先动，理由后补。**真正的投资者训练，是学会识别哪些是因果，哪些是字幕。**
---
## 二、隐藏的系统性真相：60/40组合的"复活"，意味着我们正在告别一个时代
讲者顺带提了一句被大多数听众忽略的话：**2008年之

item.title: [Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] 141. Freda的投资札记第2集：Tokenmaxxing、把电机塞进蒸汽机、接力赛变篮球赛、孤独、人的连接
item.dc:creator: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.content:encoded:
💡 创造性洞见

一、Token 是 AI 时代的"千瓦时"，但它正在重演工业革命最大的认知陷阱

Freda 把 dollar per token 类比为工业时代的 dollar per kWh，这个比喻精彩，但她自己揭示的反讽更深：今天投资人以"烧了多少 token"作为景气度指标，恰恰等同于一百年前有人炫耀"我家用电量很大"——这是把成本当成价值的典型误读。

让我意外的不是这个观察本身，而是它与 Jevons 悖论的反向呼应：当蒸汽机效率提升时，煤炭总消耗反而暴涨。Freda 给出的预言是"右下两项（token/task 和 $/token）都会大幅下降，但前两项（用户数、任务数）的爆炸会盖过一切"——这意味着 AI 不会出现 Jevons 悖论的简单复刻，而是一个"效率提升 + 单位价格暴跌 + 任务定义本身被重新发明"的三重叠加。这是工业革命没有出现过的结构。

二、"把电机塞进蒸汽机的位置"——这一集最值钱的隐喻其实指向投资本身

电力发明后 40 年生产率没提升，因为工厂只是把电机塞进蒸汽机原来的位置，没有重新设计流水线。Freda 用这个史实解释为什么 AI 现在还没真正提升企业生产率。

但她没有说破的是：这个隐喻同样适用于投资行业自己。 她讲到给 agent 喂足数据就能跑赢人类、event-driven 交易将"在几秒内"完成、卖方分析师的财务对比工作将被 AI 吞掉——可整个投资行业的组织结构（基金经理、研究员、合规、销售）仍然是前 AI 时代为人类信息瓶颈而设计的金字塔。换句话说，VC 们正在投 Neolabs，但 VC 自己就是那个"把电机塞进蒸汽机位置"的工厂。能否预测哪些基金会先死，本质上不是看他们投了什么，而是看他们敢不敢拆掉自己的中层翻译层。

三、最反常识的跨领域连接：孤独感、API key 焦虑、与"信息式关系"的崩塌

节目尾声那段关于"装不上 OpenCode"的崩溃，看起来是花絮，其实是全集最锋利的系统性洞察——只是 Freda 自己也没完全意识到。

她在前面用了大量篇幅论证：SaaS 的订阅模型不再适配智能层，因为智能是按效果按使用付费的。然后在结尾她说：以后见人不再为了交换信息，因为 95% 的信息 AI 都能更快给。

把这两件事放在一起看，会出现一个让人脊背一凉的推论：人类关系本身长期也是一种 SaaS 订阅模型——你花固定的时间成本（"维系"），换取一个对方"随时可能产出有用信息"的接入权。AI 之于人际关系，等同于按效果付费之于 SaaS：信息型友谊（"行业打听一下"）会迅速贬值并坍塌，留下的只有无法被 token 化的部分——情绪、勇气、遗憾、火花。

这解释了为什么硅谷集体焦虑伴随着集体孤独：不是 AI 让人不想社交，而是 AI 让"以信息为媒介的社交契约"第一次出现了便宜得多的替代品。就像电报普及后，写信问候的人际语法整整消失了一代人才重建。所以 Freda 在 Palo Alto 那次"没准备就坐下来聊的"对话之所以是几年来最高密度的——因为那是她第一次进入"后信息时代"的人类关系范式。

这也是为什么我倾向于反驳她对"长期乐观"的乐观本身：真正的痛苦不会来自失业，而来自一整代人需要重新学习"什么才是值得交换的东西"。这件事，比 capex 转负、比 Mag7 被稀释、比软件公司估值腰斩，都要慢，也都要深。

📝 转录文本

Freda 投资札记第 2 集

Tokenmaxxing、把电机塞进蒸汽机、接力赛变篮球赛、孤独、人的连接

Hello everyone, I'm Xiaojun. Today is our second episode of the series, "Freda's Investing Diary".

Maybe some of you are listening to our show for the first time. I'd like to introduce you to the program. Freda Duan is a partner of Altimetry Capital. This is a shareholder of a stock technology fund that invests in both the primary and secondary markets. The investment cases in the primary market include OpenAI and Anthropic. 二级市场的投资案例有英伟达、存储芯片、Snowflake、Robinhood。

In the first episode of the Freda's Investing Diary, which is our 125th episode, she thoroughly analyzed the current American star companies, calculated their huge income, and explored the new order of American capital, and the bubble topic that everyone was very concerned about at the time.

What can be foretold is that in this episode, we will talk about very dry content such as AI and investment, such as token maxing, 人和人之间啊……

Today we have our 125th episode guest, Freda, return to the studio. So, Freda, let's say hello to our audience first.

Hello, everyone. Thank you for inviting me, Xiaojun.

一、Token：AI 时代的新计量单位

Let's start with the model companies that we are familiar with, the three-party model companies. Let's talk about a new marketplace in the AI era, which is tokens. Now everyone is talking about the income and consumption of token companies, but how should we understand the essence of tokens?

I think because of AI, there are several such units that have been released in recent years. Including what we often call dollar per flop, dollar per watt, and dollar per token. Now, more and more are dollar per token. It is a comparison of the return on actual business.

I think a more obvious trend is that when we go to a company in the future, we will compare some companies. I think we will all go to see dollar per token. I will imagine it as the industrial era. Everyone looks at this dollar per kilowatt hour, which is a concept of a thousand-watt stone. The comparison is the energy consumption output ratio.

But at the same time, I've written some articles and I really want to say that I think tokens are actually a very easy to mislead unit. Because the most important thing in it is token per task. That is, you have the same task, different models or different applications, and the token consumed may be tens or even hundreds of times less. I think this understanding is very important.

为什么 token 容易误导

Why is that? There are many reasons.

  • One is that the output length can be very different. For example, if you have a task to do coding, a better model may have a hundred lines of code, and the very sophisticated code is finished. A cheaper model may need to write thousands of lines of very sloppy code. On the surface, the tasks on both sides are completed, but the token you consume is not a quantity at all.
  • There are some more hidden reasoning tokens in this. Many models will do a lot of intermediate reasoning before answering. You can't see this part of the user, but in fact, different models will consume a very different number of compute tokens.
  • Third, as we've mentioned, the Agented workflow will increase the token consumption like an amplifier.

So it's really the same task. The token consumption of different models can be very, very different.

Does this explain why some tokens are very large and the more tokens consumed, the better?

This is definitely not the case. And I think it's the other way around. Because in the end, everyone will realize that what you want is actually your effect. and you pursue the highest efficiency possible.

For example, I'll give you an example of feedback from the developers around me. Like the Cursor coding model. If you ask it the same question, it will consume more tokens. Then I will infer that the employees like it very much. and we can also see some improvements in the labor productivity. As a CFO, my conclusion is that I want to continue to add orders.

I think this is a phenomenon that the industry is in a relatively early stage. I think we still don't really understand that tokens and tokens are not the same thing. That's the fact.

Token 爆炸会自然停下来吗？

So will the so-called token explosion naturally stop? Because after all, the model will become more and more efficient.

I don't think it's that easy to answer directly whether it is or not. If we compare this with academic, there may be such a more academic formula.

The total investment of AI can actually be divided into several inputs: - The first is how many users you have, - then you use this to set up how many tasks each user has completed. - Then you set up the token per task, - and then you set up the dollar per token concept.

The first two concepts, which are the number of users and the number of tasks each user has completed, will definitely continue to grow. From programmers to ordinary white-collar workers, using AI is actually a very big wave of user rights. For example, Anthropic has a product called Cowork. So the first two items, which are the number of users and the number of tasks for each user, I don't think there's anything to say about it. It will definitely continue to grow and greatly grow.

But the latter two, I think, will have an advantage. Because now the whole situation, you see, everyone goes to say how many tokens I used and how much money I spent. I think this state itself is actually not very normal.

How do you think this will change in the future?

I think it's better to look at it this way. For example, tokens per task, just like what we just talked about, I think it's the most efficient model. In fact, overtime will be recognized by everyone.

There are some changes and specific understandings in the $/Token model. For example, the price of Anthropic is $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens. But according to the third party analysis of this industry, it actually only costs one dollar. Because it's very high in the rate of return on cash. Maybe 98% to 99% of the time. Of course, there are other reasons. For example, its identity workflow input is much longer than output.

So there are actually many articles in the "Dollar Per Token" section. I think in a while, people will realize that the cost of the model is the most important thing in this.

So how to evolve? I think it's definitely a very wasted state now. But it doesn't mean that after we finished these few, the total investment of AI will fall.

My own thoughts or the way I think is that I think the current era is very close. A lot of things are actually right and wrong. For example, you say that now everyone says that there is a lot of waste in token marketing. I think this answer is certain. But you say that the use of tokens will continue to rise significantly in the future. I think this is also right. Listening to contradictions is actually not contradictory.

I see some investors use the consumption of tokens as a basis for the judgment of the project. What do you think?

I think it's a stage product. It's like when you were in the industrial era, you wouldn't really show off that I have a light and I have a lot of power consumption. Instead, you would buy an expensive LED. I think this thing will change over time.

AI spend 真的在创造价值吗？

I remember you are good at accounting. Now many companies spend money on AI. Do you think it's really creating value or is it spending less money?

What everyone is talking about in this is actually Meta. There are rumors that it has several billion in the year of cloud code. It's a very scary number.

And then in this, the interesting thing is that Meta recently said it would pay 10% of its employees. I calculated it and said that the 10% of its employees are probably equivalent to the cost of its cloud code. But I'm not saying this is a direct cause-and-effect relationship.

For example, last month, Uber's CFO also jumped out and said that their company spent the entire year of the AI budget on coding within three months. So it's about tens of millions of this volume.

I calculated the first few companies, especially those who are doing token maxing, and their AI spend is about 1% to 2% of EBITDA. So it's still a controllable number. To be honest, the number calculated is better than I thought. I thought it was actually a bigger number.

按 token 付费的模式合理吗？

Do you think it's reasonable to use the token payment model?

I think this is a strong judgment. I think the industry is gradually becoming rational. So the payment model must change. I think it will gradually change from the payment according to the token to the payment according to the effect. Because as we just said, tokens are not comparable.

There is a company called Sierra, which is an AI customer service. And then he is completely based on the effect of the fee. And then we talked about it. In fact, his customers also very much agree with his kind of fee model. He said, if you, uh, I can help you solve this customer service, I didn't transfer the labor, I was paid. If I transfer it, I won't charge.

And there are many more problems in it. This complexity, this resolution, and then his unit price will be different. So as a client, Sierra and I are very interest-aligned. We both want to solve this problem, and we both want to burn less tokens.

So I think it's the best solution. But this is very okay in the customer service, because you can optimize. But it's not every industry. So I think it may be a little bit like the resolution of customer service, from your sales transformation, including what to charge loans or insurance compensation. I think some scenes will start to become cost-effective.

And then there are some more creative ones, such as writing. I think it may be right. For a long time, it may still be the effect of buying tokens and receiving fees. Because the writing effect is not easy to polish.

二、模型公司：Anthropic、OpenAI、XAI、Meta

Next, we will talk about the key model companies.

Anthropic 的领先优势能持续多久？

Let's start with the company that everyone is most concerned about in the past few months, Anthropic. It should be one of the most outstanding model companies this year. In your opinion, how long do you think the leading advantage of this wave of Anthropic can last? Is its range high?

I've been thinking about this question too. In the past two years, everyone's last wish is that SOTA's model will change hands every few months. Last year, everyone was talking about Gemini. And before that, it was OpenAI. Now it's Anthropic. Will it continue to change in a few months?

So the word "focusing" has been a rolling basis for the past year or two. But I think it's probably the first time in the past two months that I've questioned whether this hypothesis will continue.

The core reason is that after the coding agent was mature, a very important loop actually appeared. is that you need better AI to train the next generation of better AI. So once this cycle runs, there's a little bit of what the researchers call recursive self-improvement.

If it really passes a certain threshold, this curve will become very shaky. Then you have a little bit of singularity behind you, or how do you define it? The person chasing after you may really be meaningless.

When do you think this turning point will happen? It's been two years since AI training. Why is it different this time?

I think it's just a time period. Maybe it starts from January this year. The turning point is the maturity of coding agents. Before June, the model of coding may only have a 5% chance of improving the total factor productivity of an engineer. and then at the end of last year, it could have reached 15% or 20%. Now it's definitely higher.

So you see, the speed of new models coming out of the model factory is not like before, which is to spend a few months or even a year, but a month to release a new version. I think this is actually the real reason why a company as big as Meta is willing to spend hundreds of billions on a model from another company. Because if you don't have this thing, the speed you stack up is really slower than others.

So do you think today we are at a point where we can't chase after it anymore?

I personally think that it's already very, very fast. It's not that appropriate, but if you compare it to a car, it's a bit like, I don't know, like the feeling of changing a car in a carriage. I guess the first car that was created was also often a horse. So the front speedcars might still be able to chase after you. At that time, the gap between the two may not be so clear. But once the car can run normally and steadily, then you don't have the meaning of the horse of the engine of the car.

Codex vs. Cloud Code

So, back to coding, OpenAI has been doing a lot of regrouping in the last two months, according to GBT and coding. And they pushed coding to a very high position. Google is personally managing coding. Meta is also pushing its own coding model. And they hope to do SOTA at the end of the year. XAI is publicly acquiring Cursor. The logic behind it is actually similar.

How do you see the competition between Codex and Cloud Code?

Yes, there is a lot of comparison and discussion. I personally think that if there is a lot of debate between two products or even two people, it usually shows that their actual effect is not much different.

Codex has been obviously fighting for price recently. They made a huge supplement. And then everything, for example, Cloud Code is not allowed to be used. And then Codex is just welcome for you to use it. So I think this price is still a pretty good strategy for him. Because he is a later person after all.

This also speaks to my own reflection. It's just that these two years have taken too much time to compare open and private. But I think the more important logic is that we know that both of them will be very big.

And one of the recent thoughts is that if you look at the business model, the whole model layer, or called intelligence, it is not a SaaS concept. SaaS is subscription. It is paid by the head. When you have a subscription, the price is actually the peak. And the more you use it, the cheaper it is. So as a customer, you tend to use only one. Because you spent all your money on this per se.

But the model—Yes, I think there are many reflections in the past two years, but this is actually a very big reflection.

Coding 市场规模的误判

To be honest, when I first came out of the coding scene a year or two ago, the investors were calculating the market size of TAM, which is the price-to-value ratio. At that time, I thought that the quantity, that is, who will use this coding model, everyone said that there were many developers in the US, and there were 4-5 million people. In fact, it is a very small group of people.

When people were considering the price, I remember we had some internal discussions. Some people said that 20 knife is enough. Some people said that it should be 200 knife. Because a whole software is so expensive. Because a company spends the most money, maybe one or two thousand yuan on all software subscriptions. So 200 knife, right? Give it to coding, everyone thinks it's okay. Right?

You use the price to calculate. In fact, it is a hundred billion dollars. Such a market size. But obviously, this is completely wrong. Because until today, the income of your entire family is much more than this amount. So if you think about it, I think the price and price of the stock at that time were very wrong.

So how should this be thought about?

Yes, I think... Maybe it's really back to what Dario said, the global white collar. He has been in his PPT, the first page of the investment PPT is the white collar. He said it was a market size of about 3-40 trillion, which is about 3-40 trillion. He said this is the size of the market he wants to eat.

I understand this logic because we do a lot of things every day. There is a little bit, that is, if I will write the code, I should let the computer do the task automatically. So this idea is that my real time coding is actually anything that can be operated by the computer. And as the world is getting more and more digitalized, this range is expanding. Including now, we use AI to operate Excel, or I think we will use AI to do transactions. So this is its real time.

So every time I think about the product of coworking, it's really amazing to see two people doing it.

Back to coding, I think, or to reflect on investment, I think investment is often wrong. As an investor, I think what I can do is to realize my original investment frame and time when I quickly scored $1 billion when I found out about the coding income. Then I can make a timely change.

When did you realize it?

This is probably the earliest thing to look at. At first it was Cursor. Cursor's coding income soon reached $1 billion. Then there was CloudCo and then Clouco. At that time, because you had earned 10% of the $1 billion according to our time, it was definitely not that normal. So at that time, there should be a timely reflection.

反向滚雪球与训练-推理的约束

I remember you mentioned a concept called "reversed snowball". You used the "reversed snowball" to explain the business model of the model company. Have you made any changes to this framework?

Oh, this concept is actually mentioned by Dario. I just translated it into a more investment-oriented language. I still think this is a very classic concept.

To put it simply, if you trained a model last year and the cost was 1, then this model can bring about 3-4% of the income this year. Then the profit is 50%, which is less than 2 yuan.

So the company didn't think about the demand being so strong.

Yes, yes, yes. Dario has actually made the prediction very extreme and very beautiful. It's a very first-class principle.

But there is a very simple formula here. There are two things. - One is that if the cost of your income is lower than the profit you push, the company can make money. - The second is a constraint. The total cost of training plus reasoning is actually pre-ordered.

I don't know if I made myself clear. For example, the company can control the profit margin of your reasoning. But the proportion of training and reasoning is ultimately determined by your needs. So your needs are not really controllable. But for example, the needs of the beginning of the year suddenly burst. So the company will be forced to put more constraints on reasoning. So suddenly there was violence.

怎么看 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的收入对比

Now the media often report the income of companies like OpenAI and Anthropic. How do you compare these two?

It's hard to compare. First of all, everyone calls it ARR, but it's not ARR at all. Because ARR is called Annual Recurring Revenue. In the model, it's not annual, it's not recurring. And the scope of use is really 7 or 8, everything has. Some use the income of the past four weeks to multiply by 13. And then the more radical use the past week to multiply by 53. So in fact, it can be a lot of difference.

And these two companies didn't make it easy to compare. Some used gross, some used net. For example, Anthropic reported gross revenue. And you have to make some adjustments. For example, if 40% is the third party API, then you need net. You have to deduct this and reduce it by 20%. And OpenAI is the opposite. It reports a net.

How do you see OpenAI and Anthropic's demand for compute?

I think the biggest point I learned from this is that the market, including myself, actually underestimated how much model income each gigawatt power could generate.

The third party reported that OpenAI and Anthropic now have a few gigawatt power. And at the end of the year, it should be up to 5, and next year, for example, up to 10. Before, everyone thought that each gigawatt could almost correspond to, for example, a 100 billion income is not bad, but these few months have proven that this number has been greatly underestimated.

The cost of reasoning and training should be corresponding to each gigawatt. It's okay to have a 100 billion, but the income side can actually be marked very high. So the "in-pride" of this thing, the "in-pride" of model companies' revenue, I think it's actually underestimated by the system.

How much income can a gigawatt generate? No one can see the upper limit today. You said one billion, you said five billion. In fact, it seems quite possible now.

On topic, what time do most stock investors see this company?

I think the real inflation point of Anthropic was last year. It was when its ARR was from 1 billion to 5 billion. At that time, the head was because it used less than half a year from 10 to 50. And it's much faster than the same period of OpenAI.

Actually, when you see that, you should already know, right? Like we just mentioned, for example, coding is bigger and faster than we thought. But at that time, it was not out of the question. At that time, there were two things that were out of the question. One is that the profit margin was very ugly at that time. But some people who work in infrastructure and engineering may think that it's not a big problem and there are many advantages to it. And another thing that everyone was very concerned about was the relationship between Dario and the US government.

But I think most investors in 2025 will think that coding is really something that needs to be improved or improved.

三家会长期同台吗？

Do you think OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini will compete in the same river for a long time? Or will they be divided?

This is also interesting. I think last year, I would think that some companies have already had obvious division. It's almost settled. But this year, I think the model business model may be quite different. What will live up to what income everyone will do.

I used to think that GPT is very addictive. I put my tax returns or medical bills in a folder called Projects. But this year, just because Anthropic has a visualization function that can help me draw pictures, I'm actually down.

That's also because people think that the coding market is going to be huge. So they don't really care about the current specific criteria. They just think that the model layer itself will take up a larger proportion of Compute Stack. And then at the end of the year, these two families add up to see what a 21 billion income volume—I think it's not particularly strange. So it's also about training your brain to get used to bigger numbers, and this is called upside surprises.

XAI 与 Meta TBD

What about XAI, which is based on VASC and FUNMULINI?

To be honest, I don't think anyone can make any judgment on whether XAI will succeed or not. Because there are no data points that can be used. There is no team, no progress. It's really a mess.

But there is one thing about it. For example, Iran rents the XCI data center to Anthropic. I saw many media saying that it is completely out of training. It will be a Neo Cloud. This is not true. This is all public data.

But XCI has three data centers. There is Colossus, there is Macro Hard, and the third one is called Macro Harder. The two behind it are all using Blackwell. And Colossus uses H100, which is originally used for push. So it's actually a non-useful one, and rent it to the most lacking one, Anthropic. And that one itself is not big, just 300 megawatts. Maybe it's like this, XAI has a total data center of 20%, and it can rent out about 4-5 billion in revenue. So it's actually a very make sense business strategy.

What do you want to say about Meta TBD?

For Meta TBD, I think one is that it will definitely hit a SOTA model at the end of the year. It may be a Gemini 2.5 today. And then the bigger problem is how Meta will really transform after it makes this model.

And now it seems that there are so many possible ways. - One is the Meta AI that it has been pushing, which is a separate app. - And then Meta should also push their own Open Cloud or the little lobster assistant. - And then the third one, I would also be curious, I would take a look, because it is now using its coding model internally, but if the coding model can really be done, can that part have some income for the outside?

I think this is the point of Meta, right? Is there really an AI income that can be added?

三、软件公司：人效震撼与"消失的销售"

AI has also been a hot topic in the discussion of the share market. What do you think about this?

The biggest reaction I've received is that Software is a different thing from 2C. It's a push model. You need sales and a whole set of go-to-market. So whether it's ServiceNow or Adobe, there are 30,000 to 40,000 people. Salesforce has 70,000 to 80,000 people. A large part of it is sales. The sales and marketing ratio should be around 40% to 50%.

The biggest shock is that Anthropic is also a 2B company. 80% of its users are entrepreneurs, and only 3,000 employees. In traditional software companies, you can calculate that each employee has a revenue of $500,000. Anthropic employees should have a revenue of tens of millions of dollars. It's just a number of employees. And Anthropic doesn't have a serious sales team. Whether it's co-working, skills, or projects, but it's not that easy for ordinary people to get these products. But everyone is still going crazy to learn and use them.

So I'm also reflecting on whether users really have unlimited needs for intelligence. Is it that we were a little too much in the past to emphasize the importance of sales?

I think this is not just about software. Because it will also make people reflect on why we need so many employees in big companies like WeChat. This contrast is not just for software companies, right?

Yes, for example, Meta is already a very efficient company with 80,000 employees. And each employee has a profit of $300. It's much better than software. But after AI appeared, I think it's amazing to see 800,000 employees. This is a 2C company. There are no new customers. There must be some maintenance. But why is the entire team of friends still in a state of busyness?

So there are a lot of things to reflect on. I think it's still like what I said earlier, such as probably a co-work, right? Such a successful product is made by two people. So after AI came out, I think it's worth thinking about what the organizational structure should look like. And then how many people does a company need?

投资人是否在抛弃软件公司？

So are investors abandoning software companies?

Yes, simply put, yes. I think there is a very simple logic in investment, which is that we look at the discount rate. Because at the end of the day, many things are what you may have heard of as DCF, which is to look at the future cash flow, and then you put a discount rate, and then count it today, and then see how much the company is worth. So it doesn't need to be a very strong judgment on the future. As long as the uncertainty rises, the valuation will be suppressed, because the discount rate will rise.

So there's another interesting observation in the second-tier market. In fact, the software market has already fallen by more than 50% from the previous 788. But in the first-tier market, there are still many companies that remain in the valuation logic before AI. So there will be some first and second-tier valuation traps. Many celebrity companies in the first-tier market, whether it's Databricks or some big and good companies, if you go public today, your valuation will be cut quite heavily.

Yes, and the software company has another problem, which is its SBC. If your stock price drops by 50%, and you still want to give the employees the same compensation, you will have to pay twice the amount. So it's a relatively difficult situation.

哪些软件最脆弱？

Which software do you think is more vulnerable?

I think it's all about time. For example, I would look at the level of UI in the software itself. If the core value is a very exquisite UI, it is the weakest and the most vulnerable. For example, many point solutions are the most thoroughly impacted. Whether it's electronic signatures, project management, or some BI tools.

And then it also depends on how much it is structured in terms of data. You may have heard that all software is an Excel wrapper. But actually, there are many differences between Rapper and Rapper. For example, Monday is a software that uses project management tools. Its data structure is very regular. Each line is a fixed task object, and each row is fixed. This type of task is actually the easiest to replace by an agent.

But Excel is actually very confusing. Each grid in each table has a completely different language. No matter what your name is, your number, your company name, they're all mixed up. So I think Excel itself is very difficult to replace by AI directly. So I think it's also a matter of time. Which ones are in front of you?

Office 软件还有意义吗？

Which ones do you think can be held up? Is the office software of Office still meaningful?

Yes, recently, people have been talking about this a lot. They say that the whole Office, whether it's PowerPoint or Excel, doesn't make any sense anymore. You can just use a website's HTML.

I used to think so, but I think it's not that simple. It's like AI creating a web version of PowerPoint is actually very fast. But as long as you need to manually change it, you will find that you can still change it directly in PowerPoint. If you change it directly by hand, it will be faster and more accurate.

I would imagine the whole Office 7 as a flow of water line. I may not need to manually edit it, I just need to review it. But as long as I send this file to the entire flow of water line, as long as there is one person who needs to manually edit it, I think the Office 7 tool will actually continue to have the value of existence.

数据仓库真的安全吗？

What kind of software is relatively safe?

The market will generally think that data storage and data warehouse are relatively safe. But recently, we are all using this in detail. And I have an idea that I think this may not be absolute.

For example, as an investment manager, I want to pull, for example, the valuation of 50 stocks. Make a quick comparison. In the past, I needed a warehouse to host this data. Then I will write a very accurate SQL to call. But now if I have this model and I write a skill in the cloud and I directly rely on the Bloomberg API, I only need a few seconds of token consumption to complete an analysis. Then my setup cost is almost zero. This is completely impossible before the model comes out.

So it makes me think about whether the warehouse or the data lake or something like that is purely safe. I don't think so.

AI 时代的新软件机会

Where are the new software opportunities in the age of AI? From a VC perspective, many companies say they are AI CRM, or AI ERP. But it seems that there is no real change in nature. In other words, they don't need to manually input, they can input for you.

I think there are many people talking about it recently, but I think a bigger opportunity is to let software handle the parts that were not recorded in the past in the role of these companies.

For example, if I record a customer in CRM, I will record that I have given this customer a 25% discount. But today's CRM doesn't record why it's 20% or 30%. There were some plans that were denied by someone, who convinced who, right? What concerns did the CFO have? What does the client want? All these decision-making processes are actually gone.

This thing couldn't be recorded before because there was too much unstructured data. In the era of AI, you don't need to use a compression tool anymore. You can deal with the more primitive information directly. So it's a... There might be a bigger chance here, I think.

软件是否需要为 agent 重新设计？

Do you think software needs to be redesigned for agents?

I think so. It's also a recent use. For example, one is to connect to Slack and Discord. And then I found that Slack is based on HTTP. You send a message to a person and his server responds. It's good for human chat, but it's very, very, very, very difficult for agents. Because Agent is very long-term, real-time, and needs to be online for a long time. And I found that Discord, because it's a WebSocket, is very easy to use.

So I was even thinking that if any model company bought Discord, it wouldn't be strange. So I think the whole software set must be designed from the first principle, what does Agent need to do a new design together?

It's even more obvious in the stock market. Everyone is talking about the shortcomings of CPUs. In fact, it's almost the same thing.

四、组织结构：把电机塞进蒸汽机的位置

I saw that you wrote a lot about AI-era organizational architecture. This is a bit different from the topics that investors will study, right? Why did you start studying this?

Yes, I'm not an HR or a COO. But in my research, I think this is much more important than you think. And it's actually very interesting.

Dario has mentioned two concepts. He said one is called technology diffusion, and the other is called economic diffusion. Technology means the improvement of model capabilities. You can see that this curve is very steep. The economic diffusion means that the model capacity is absorbed by the enterprise and the economy at a speed that is impossible to be measured. It may take years to be measured, even decades.

So, the research on this organizational structure is essentially to judge when AI becomes income and when the productivity of the society will increase.

历史先例：电力与计算机

Are there any similar examples in history?

Oh, there are actually very, very, very classic examples.

One is when electricity was invented. It took 40 years for electric bulbs to really increase the production rate in society. In about 20 or 30 years, the production rate did not increase at all, but it even decreased a little. And the reason behind this is that, um, it's not really used on the flow line. It doesn't really improve efficiency. Everyone still removed the steam engine and changed it. Then put the electric machine in the same position. Then the factory is still designed like the original vertical design. It has been formed on the flow line. In fact, the production rate of that era is only going up.

So it's also a big shock to me. That is to say, new technology appears. It's really not directly equivalent to this economic diffusion or the value of this economy.

Another thing that is closer to us is that when it comes to computers, it should be the 80s, 90s, 80s, computers were already in offices and banks. At that time, it was very similar to what it is now. I think everyone feels that their work is faster. But the production rate on the red line—I think it's probably like the electric machine, the steam engine, the position of the steam engine.

Everyone has added their own workflow to AI. But I don't think anyone really asked about the process itself or why the company itself has grown to be like this. Or like we just said, why does it take 80,000 people to build a company? Why do we need so many levels? There are still a lot of unresolved problems.

公司的层级本质上是信息传递

So from the perspective of the first-person perspective, why do you think the company is like this today?

The company actually has a few things. You have a lot of people, and you have a lot of levels today, and then there's some information flow in the middle.

In general, everyone says that the level is understood as a structure of power, who reports to whom, and who is senior to whom. But I think this level is actually more profound. It should be a message transmission. Because when your organization grows, no one can see the full picture. So you need a management layer by layer. Then it collects signals from the CEO first. Then it integrates, then it trains, then it transmits it layer by layer. Then it translates and disassembles from the bottom to the top in this way.

I think the mechanism of organization is to solve the problem of information transmission, including the meetings we hold, the synchronization process, and the one-quarter meeting. All of these are actually a mechanism of information transfer, because the number of people who communicate with each other is relatively high.

Is this particularly obvious in technology companies?

Yes, or you can think of a product that was designed in a tech company. You need to write the PRD, and your designer needs to interpret the PRD into a tangible thing. And the developer needs to do a few weeks or months of development. And then there's a few weeks of QA. And then you have the go-to-market, the sales team. And then it's probably six months after the end.

A lot of it is actually translation costs. It's like one person translates what the other person wants to do into something that everyone can understand.

I think every part of AI that comes in will become a new bottleneck. I heard a founder say that the most interesting thing was the time when the developer started to develop. You might have to wait two to three months before that. You have a vibe code now, and you may have finished two weeks. Then you will find that QA is not right, it becomes bottleneck. You have no reason to spend a few weeks on QA. So you just remove the QA person or change the function. Then you will find that the previous PM design has become a new bottleneck. It becomes the place that takes the most time. Then after you change it, you find that the go-to-market has become the latest bottleneck. So it's like solving problems again and again. I think the whole process actually needs to be redesigned.

从接力赛到篮球赛

After your research, what do you think will happen after the redesign?

I think it will be a relay competition that will be one step at a time. And then it will be more like a small team basketball game. Maybe just three to five people in a small team. And then your necessary skills are in your team. And this team should be able to make their own decisions. And then only very big problems will go up. report, maybe it's a model like this. Then some details in it, such as your QA, may be involved in development, and then your PM needs to be more comprehensive. Yes, so there are a lot of changes in details.

I'm also looking for organizations that are moving faster, but I haven't seen any in the US yet.

Consensus will say that the middle level will be compressed. I also feel that the amount of information that can be traced in a company will increase. I think people will capture the whole process of negotiation between him and the client and will feed it to the agent. And the agent can get all the historical data of my CRM, and then the network. Then he should be able to have a God-like perspective, and can tell me directly what the client is in fact, and what you need to do. So the overall feeling is that the enterprise is more alive. But I'm looking for a better example.

五、AI 与投资行业自身

You see, we talked about tokens, models, software, and organizations. Now we will go back to your most familiar field, which is what changes will the investment industry of AI bring?

I think there are many reasons. Because the investment industry is really a very small industry. Everyone spends a lot of time looking for information, and then cleaning up the data, and then comparing the expectations, and then judging the positioning.

The problem I've been thinking about these months is that if you give this agent enough data and enough data to tell him clearly whether his trading goal is better than others. I think 100% of the data will be better than others. Of course, you need to tell them what your ideal trading is like, because everyone has their own ideal trading or trading style. How long do you want to hold each one? How much profit do you want to pursue? How much return can you take? What kind of opportunity would you like to take? After all these definitions, I think AI can create a very perfect trading system.

So I personally think that it didn't come out today because everyone's thinking is very confused, right? They didn't fully understand their own thinking process.

You didn't do it today. What do you think is the main difficulty?

I think one is obviously data. Because financial data sounds very standardized, but it's actually very, very, very fragile. For example, recently, I was using this agent to pick up financial data. I found that I need to pick up maybe more than ten or twenty vendors of financial data to be able to collect the data I usually use. So it's a very messy industry.

And then I think it's also necessary to better understand the different players behind the market.

美股的玩家结构与散户偏好

怎么去做决策吧？就是拿美股来说，从交易量的角度来看，其实量化占比是超过百分之六七十的。然后散户大概是30%。然后传统机构我们觉得很大，但实际上根本不占什么交易量。然后平台型的一些机构，比如什么 Citadel 啊，它能占个百分之，就是个位数吧。然后如果你再回到量化，如果你假设高频本身，它不去下一个方向性的一个判断的话，它更像是一个 momentum 的一个加强器，那所以真正影响方向的就是散户、中频和 platform 上。

所以说回来就是 What does an A-hunter like?

I think there are a few rules that I have clearly summarized. - One is that the whole of the retail industry actually likes PE, which is relatively low. And then maybe not necessarily look at so much very detailed financial data, but how can PE numbers be so good? - And then the other is that they like dreams that are relatively big and like these founders with courage, including these years, this Tesla, SpaceX, etc. including Robinhood, - and also those who prefer the absolute low stock price. For example, if you have 8 yuan per share, you will receive a higher discount than 800 yuan per share.

But in the end, I think retail investors like the stock price. Anyway, I feel that the future is not an institution that educates retail investors, but an institution that learns from retail investors. Because the market is becoming more and more stylistic, more and more thematic, and more and more narrative driven. So retail investors are part of your price.

市场会变得波动更大还是更有效率？

Do you think the market will change a lot?

I think so. One is the gap between retail and institutional. I think it will be very fast. And then I think basically with the polarization, especially with the polarization of the middle class, I think there will be a wave called Converge. And then I think any market with the realization of the alpha will become very fast.

For example, if OpenAI announced cooperation with a company, the market would have to spend a few days to understand. Within these days, I would call different investment friends of different institutions, and let them check the numbers. You will see that the stock price will be adjusted to the position that most of our players think is reasonable. But then, you give the agent all this information, he can go and see some previous cases by himself. So he can reach that stock price in that second. So this event-driven, event-driven trading, I think it will be more and more, it's almost unnecessary.

For example, in financial reports, we need to look at many things. You need to calculate the data from the third party, ask for market expectations, and compare consensus. I think these will be more detailed and more framework-based in the future. AI will have a sense of God's perspective and make more accurate judgments.

How will the market change? I think maybe because of the very accurate changes made by agents, the stock price may not have such a big fluctuation in performance as it does today.

Do you think the market will fluctuate more or will it be more efficient?

Both. Because the market will fluctuate more because everyone reacts faster. Because the Asian market will magnify almost the same signal, the transaction will be more crowded. And it will be more efficient because the information will quickly be reflected in the price.

So the real alpha will be from "I found it earlier than you" to "I can understand some big trends better than you". And I think it's obvious that cross-industrial research is becoming more and more important.

I think we need to make a patent in the investment industry in the future. For example, some of our funds today say that they mainly do this kind of small and medium-sized companies, which is a small company that no one sees, because there are some alphas in it. But I think this thing will soon be covered by AI, because AI has a very strong learning ability. So I'm looking forward to seeing a lot of changes.

六、应用层与新创公司

Let's go back to the investment level. What directions has the AI live streaming startup company taken to make a profit? You can ask a quick question.

Oh, coding is the biggest one. It's the biggest one. And then it's broken down. - For example, healthcare, whether it's a bridge, open evidence, it's about two dollars. - And then legal, whether it's Harvey or LaGuardia, it's more than one dollar. - Customer services is close to one billion. - Video generation is about hundreds of millions. - AI is the reasoning for the infrastructure, whether it's together or fireworks. This industry has a few billion of revenue. - And then for the chip, whether it's Cerebras that is about to go public, or the Grok that was acquired before, this is also the big one.

This almost covers all the startup companies that can reach a billion.

大模型公司会直接吃掉创业公司吗？

Will these model giants compete with them directly in these fields?

This is actually quite interesting. This question has been asked since 2022, but I think the answer has changed over the past few years.

In 2023, when everyone called this AI wrapper or model wrapper, everyone was worried that the model company would eat everything. But in fact, in the last two years, we have stopped saying these things. Because we have found that these companies have increased their income very quickly and have some relatively good returns.

But maybe it's only been two months. I think I'm starting to worry about the competition of model companies again. Because the model itself...

Open and Unprofit announced that they will work with private fund to push the model to the companies that are in the back of the fund. There will be a big adoption in this. So, yes, there is a lot of progress.

Speaking of this, what are the remaining applications of the English company? The model company is going to open up everywhere and swallow everything.

I have my own opinion, but I want to talk about how these app companies defend themselves. For example, companies that do legal work will say that because of the regulations, the supervision of the industry is relatively strict, and the law firm is very worried that the model will cause hallucinations. So they can't use today's big model to directly make applications. And then, like we talked about, some customer service companies will emphasize that there are a lot of last mile in the customer service field. You always have some long-term needs.

But I personally think that if you are just relying on a contract or you are just dealing with the last mile of this work, you can't actually eat the biggest and fattest value. And I don't think your profit rate will be particularly good for the long term.

So in 2026, will you still be investing in the company as an investor?

I will still be investing. There is another change in the liquidity of the investment industry. I think it's also very important. In the past few months, in fact, every VC fund you are familiar with has established a growth fund, which is to invest in post-projects. Then use this fund to actually go from your VC fund to pick up large projects like OpenAI and Entropy.

So the VC fund OK.

Neolabs：硅谷最热的赛道

So what's the most popular VC in your company right now?

I think the most important thing in the past few months has been Neolabs. There are really 100 Neolabs. Neolabs are the new labs that researchers from AI Labs have opened. Yes, including Senning, Hongletong, and Suyu. They all count as Neolabs.

I also found that the investment theme of Guigu has become Neolabs. What does this mean? Why do people invest so much? And why is it at this time?

Is it true that entrepreneurs who are capable of making models don't really know how to use them? Generally speaking, the gap between the two companies is quite big.

而且再有就是这几年因为硅谷整个它叫 acquire hire 对吧，就是你只要你优秀，你总会被一个非常优秀的 lab 再去再去给招回去，所以让退出气质变得非常的好。所以我觉得这样我就主要这个是是原因吧。然后我也可以实话说，就是从 VC 的角度，因为有的 VC 就是投 Neolabs 投的非常的多，有的是完全不碰，这个背后实际上是一个——It's a very simple math question.

Because every new lab has a higher financing amount, the VC would want to say I can't invest all of them in a few big companies. But I can't invest only one company. Because you can't choose one company. So I need to invest in 4 or 5 companies. If I invest 50 million in each company, I will have an investment of about 200 million. At this time, VC will see how big my fund is. If I have a fund of hundreds of millions, I can invest in the whole NEO Labs. If we have a few times the profit, that's fine. So this is from the point of view of VC.

Agentic commerce 与 agent 基础设施

What other directions do you have besides Neolabs?

Any companies that can push the model or make it cheaper are very popular. Another interesting thing is to give these agents to start-ups that are doing basic facilities.

因为像我们刚才讲的 agent 他不是人嘛，就当他用这个工具用 email，然后包括用这个用支付和人是不一样的。不用拿 email 来说，就是你今天这个 gmail API，对发送量的这个请求的这个频率是有限制的。

How do you see this line of "agentic commerce"?

Compared to a few months ago, I think To Be is much bigger than To See. To See is a good experience for consumers. Consumers are willing to spend time on these websites and there are some entertainment properties in it. If AI just helps me place orders, it may be valuable, but it's not that big.

So you can see that OPEC might have seen this and thought it wasn't easy to do. So they are only doing the travel related part, without touching the actual consumption.

七、自动驾驶与机器人

Finally, let's talk about autonomous driving and robots. What do you think of this new trend?

To be honest, I think the overall development is slower than I thought. So this is also an improvement for me.

For example, the most interesting thing about the car is that Alpamayo, which is the most interesting thing about the car, has launched its own open source model. If this can really be done, it will be like the Android system of the car factory.

如果能成的话，行业的格局都会变。就不再只是一两家车厂能做自动驾驶，而是会很快有一个扑开的过程。所以这个是我个人花时间最多，也是觉得最值得关注的。

And then some of the details of autonomous driving, everyone started to roll reasoning. For example, the original model feels like it's already done. But the autonomous driving is that everyone started to do reasoning. Because everyone also found that you rely on data to imitate learning, you can't cover all the edge cases. So the model still has to be able to understand what happened on the road.

Are there any breakthroughs in the robot recently?

I've read a lot of books. I might be more cautious.

China—Yes, if China-US relations are better, it's a reasonable decision to invest in China.

八、美股大势：capex、IPO、与 Mag7 的稀释

We talked about the impact of AI on US stock market last year. Do you have any new thoughts in the past six months?

Yes, I also reviewed what we talked about last year. I think there are two judgments that need to be very sincere. I think one is right and one is wrong.

The right one is that at the end of last year, I think the most important trend in the market this year will be the income of OpenAI and Anthropic. That's right. I think the market is so hot today that it's a family of Anthropic's income.

But there was a very ridiculous judgment at that time. I thought that the center of the entire market this year would slowly bounce from semiconductor to AI applications. This judgment seems to be completely wrong now.

Yes, I feel that before and after the New Year, there are two worlds.

What do you think is the core judgment now?

Or I'm most worried about the big companies, especially the cloud companies, that will have a hard time with cash flow. I don't know what the market will react to this.

According to the capital spending curve, in 2027, the capital flow of the big companies will turn into debt. The entire industry has more than 10 million US dollars of capex. And these are just the numbers on the table. In fact, each major company has signed a lot of capital expenses outside the table. And these big customers have signed long-term contracts with these storage companies. So your CAPEX forecast will almost only be up-to-date, not down-to-date. So I don't know what the situation will be like when everyone really sees that it's all negative cash flow.

But the market will also ask if there is any return on the money spent.

Yes, this problem has always existed. This year, a better change is that in addition to model manufacturers, model manufacturers can even see the profit. Then, the cloud side also sees investment returns and it also begins to accelerate. For example, Google is an example. If it says this year—Why do you think the business has become worse?

One is competition. The other is that I think it's in value. It's in this Compute Stack. This value chain has also been divided a lot. Like now there are a lot of this new calls. We just talked about this, right? It's also a new new call. Then you now add up a few manufacturers. There is already this $20,000 million of this income reserve. In fact, more than half of these models are open and proper.

芯片和广告

Do you see any changes in the new industry?

For chips, many cloud companies have started to turn their chips into a commercial product for selling. Google's TPU has changed the most. It used to be sold for nothing. But now it has started to sell TPU as hardware directly to customers. I think this is the most important change in its strategy in the past 10 years.

Amazon has a revenue of over $200 billion. It's already a large independent chip company.

I remember we talked about advertising. If OpenAI starts selling ads, what impact will it have on the market?

Oh, I have a reflection on this. I think it's not fully understood before. Because my previous thought was that I think if OpenAI does advertising, it will be very dangerous for the industry. Because the online advertising rate in the United States is over 80%. Then you OpenAI comes in, you must be a storage concept. You can only grab it from Google Meta.

Then I watched Amazon's ads. In the past few years, Amazon has made almost a billion-dollar advertising business. It's not a traditional advertising budget. For example, if Bao Jie—There are not many details, but I think the whole market is still very large. And for new players, for example, when they start to sell ads, it may not be a concept that they must grab from Google's Meta.

巨型 IPO 的市场冲击

Are there any other super large IPOs in the past? What will this have a big impact on the market?

Yes, the first one is SpaceX, and the two you mentioned. These are added up. Let's say we're talking about an IPO worth $40 million. In this case, the financing is estimated to be $2 billion. And the circulation is estimated to be $4 billion.

So we've done a lot of calculations and we've been thinking about it. From a pure market capacity perspective, it's bearable. If you actively invest in stocks and funds around the world, it can have a dollar of cash in its account. Plus retail investors. Because retail investors can't invest in OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX. They will definitely want to invest in the market. Including some main fund, including hedge funds. So the market can't be stopped.

But I personally feel that the bigger problem is actually a financial turmoil. When your new IPO comes in, everyone likes it and wants to take it. I'm sure the current MAG7 will be suppressed. This doesn't count for what we just said. A lot of MAG7's cash flow has to be paid. So you were the 7th giant and then many of you will be wiped out.

Will the big vote financial source have any impact?

This has also done a lot of calculation. I think it's called EPS for it. The revenue of each stock is limited. If 20% of your employees can match 5% or 10% of the EPS of the Mag7, it will have a huge impact on software companies. For example, Salesforce, a typical human resources-intensive SaaS company, can easily match the growth of 40% or 50% of the EPS of a 20% employee.

I think there are a few possible ones. One is social impact. If there is a large-scale financial resource, the government may interfere. Then, for example, the return of the waste of tokens, such as the external model of the META billion. But I still have that point of view. I think even if there is a wave of waste, I don't think it will affect the trend in the long term.

And then there is the fact that I think everyone—It's a very good thing. But Anthropic's income is not end-customer. For example, other people use Anthropic to generate better income. This income has never been seen before. I think it's more optimistic. Maybe by the end of the year, people will want to see a real income because of AI.

In general, is Gui Gu now under control of the financial resources?

I think so. And anxiety and fear are the most common emotions. And then there's another one. I think it's very realistic. In reality, in recent years, many people have also been rich because of AI. And so it's the gap between wealth and your technology that can be put together. How big is this impact?

You mentioned talent, right? Recently, someone asked Dr. Druckenmiller if AI is definitely a cause of talent and communication. And his original answer was that if you listen to this idea, you are arrogant and not open-minded. I said that I am not qualified to make a conclusion on this. On the one hand, since human memory, I open-minded, keep an open-minded attitude.

If the pessimist is right, AI causes a large-scale employment impact, the government can also do some response through printing, or say, the general public's basic income, or say, the state budget. So it may not be a market that everyone imagined for a long time.

I think what investors can do, or what ordinary people can do, is to know what the mainstream of the market is, but at the same time, to see the path that others have not seen yet, and then to prepare psychologically in advance. So when this plot, no matter which way it is taken, it can be prepared psychologically and in this combination, it can be quickly adjusted. I think this is something that can be done.

历次技术革命对就业的冲击

How has the technological revolution affected employment in history? Do you have any research?

I actually spent a lot of time on this, so that AI can look back on hundreds of years of history, including the first manual labor that was destroyed by the power plant in the UK. And then there was the large-scale American agricultural population, from 40% of the labor to only 1%. There was also a wave in the US, where there were hundreds of thousands of people at the peak of the telephone line. In the end, all of them were replaced by automation. Including the Iron Belt, which is a little bit more recent in the US. There are also many changes that have been made in the world of China's WTO.

I don't dare to say that I really learned anything，但最大的感受是：历史上人类真的经历了很多区别。今天我们会觉得 AI 这种，对吧，很多不确定性，很大的压迫感。但如果真的和当年就工业化取代手工农业，对农民的震撼相比，我觉得是不是一个量级的。

就是每一代人，或者说每一个个体吧，都会觉得自己非常的 special，觉得自己非常的不一样。但实际上放到一个历史的长河，也就是很小的这么一个波澜。

那你觉得这次到底是一样还是不一样？

Oh, I think it's the same or different. The speed and breadth of the data are important. And then the speed and breadth of the changes.

Then your speed, historically, many technologies have been slowly transforming. For example, agricultural power has had a huge impact, but it took 100 years. Even the automation of the line source took decades. I think AI is probably going to spread much faster. And then, in terms of width, everyone thinks that this time the white collar has a huge impact. But in fact, the previous agricultural or something, the corresponding scale is actually similar.

It's really hard to judge. For example, in 2015 and 2016, Hinton said that we shouldn't have radiation doctors anymore because deep learning has exceeded the level of human radiation doctors. Today, the number of doctors has not only decreased, but also increased. And recently, everyone said that the software is dead and the company is about to close. But in fact, you see, the data of the developer's recruitment has actually reached a point where it is the best in history.

So I think it's the same or not. To be honest, let's take a step at a time. I personally think that the change will be much faster.

Are you pessimistic for a long time or optimistic for a long time?

Long term, long term is definitely optimistic. I think one thing can greatly improve productivity, long term is definitely good for the economy. I think now, specifically, I think it's a time for entrepreneurship. When these big factories are no longer safe.

But for entrepreneurship, it's easy to get a raise. And then you code to make things faster. And then there are a lot of new needs. And then the pattern is not stable. So everyone calls this the fourth industrial revolution, a great era. So it's not a shame to do something new.

Do you have any suggestions for entrepreneurs?

If you're in the industry, I don't think you should listen to my analysis. Because to be honest, the previous analysis was all big companies. The analysis is more, right? There are already very clear business model companies. These VC, especially early projects, are difficult to make you feel that there is a mutual agreement. And then there is a very, right? It's called a moat. If you think about it, it's actually not. 什么东西是模型公司或者大厂真的想做自己做不出来的，我觉得也是没有的。所以这就是建议。

九、孤独、连接与"语言世界"

前面聊了很多干货硬核的东西，包括模型啊、商业模式啊、即是一几百亿的数字。那我想换个频道啊，因为很多人都去硅谷带了一圈回来，反馈最多的就是焦虑。那你天天在那里，你的真实感受是什么？你焦虑吗？

To be honest, I'm very anxious. Maybe it's better to say it, but to be honest, I'm very anxious. I even thought about it myself. I thought about the essence of anxiety. is the uncertainty of the future. I feel like I can't keep up with the current state of things.

I've been doing auto-research, and I've also done a demo of a new workflow for Keri 10. Even with the new features on the cloud, the changes in software and hardware are too much and too fast. I think you should use technology to make your own decisions. But the truth is that there are too many things and too fast.

When I open Twitter every day, people will say, "You have to look at this. This is changing my life." You have a feeling that you have something new, but you're lagging behind and you're not following up.

I think in this era of uncertainty, to be honest, I think whether it's the media or individuals, it's actually very easy to create anxiety. You just ask how many tokens you burned this week. You have a few open clouds, right? This kind of name. Then add up the fear of the financial resources. It's really a very bad state.

装不上 OpenCode 的那个夜晚

So you also feel that you can't keep up, right?

Yes, actually, a few months ago, one day, I was so tired because I couldn't put on the Open Club at home at night. It's funny to say, but at that time, if you couldn't put on something, you would think that you couldn't even put on this, you couldn't follow anything, and you would be eliminated by the times.

To be honest, I don't even know how ordinary people can put it on at once, because the Open Club has a lot of things. And it wasn't that trivial. She asked me to write something like JSON. I couldn't even find the light on the screen. She asked me to give her an API key. You can't expose the API key. Anyway, I was very anxious and anxious at that time. It's easy to have negative emotions.

But I don't want to say this too heavily, because it's not just anxiety that I feel every day. There's also a kind of sudden excitement, like you can do anything. Because in the process of change, you have the most opportunities, and you invest in the most opportunities.

I hope our audience can feel better when they hear your example. Everyone is anxious, not just you.

Yes, I hope that after saying a lot of things, it doesn't matter.

焦虑：时代的特征

Do you think this state is a curve feature or is it a feature of our entire era?

I think it's a feature of the era. Anxiety is nothing but the first thing you care about. And then the second thing is that you as a body, you don't have enough of this sense of certainty.

People will have a kind of desire to control over future impulses. But all the issues we talked about earlier, whether there will be a financial crisis, how much the economy will be affected, and how long the "unproposal" will last, are all very uncertain topics.

I think people, including myself, will have their own opinions on everything. But there is an English saying, "Strong opinions, loosely held."

Now, because of the time we're in, you can't control your future completely. The intelligence is essentially very equal. Like today, a child in a small town can use a tool. Maybe there is no difference in nature between a nuclear engineer and a nuclear engineer. But at the same time, because of equality, many resources in the whole society will also have a new distribution. So this equality will be achieved in a very painful way in this society.

So I think we should be very lucky to have witnessed many histories in the last decades. But it seems that everyone is not as lucky as we thought. They feel like they are being driven away by anxiety.

不再想见人：信息式社交的崩塌

You also mentioned a change before, that is, you don't want to communicate with people anymore.

Yes, that's true. It's also a sense of loneliness. I used to love to talk with people, especially with industry experts. For example, within 30 minutes, I could get very high-level knowledge from a few top people in a few fields. That kind of feeling is very cool.

But now, there are many people I don't care who the person is, whether it's an entrepreneur or an investor. I can ask the AI for the answer to 95% of the information. And I might want the answer more. It's not that I have any sense of superiority in it and don't want to talk to others. I really think that the purpose of the exchange of information is to quickly get out of control.

What do you think is left between people?

Between people, it's very complicated. I think the rest is the connection of emotions. Now, for example, when I go out to a meeting, as long as I am prepared, the content of the other party's reality can actually be prepared and basically predicted.

But for example, I actually sat down with a friend in Palo Alto a while ago and maybe I wasn't prepared to talk about anything big. Then we can chat and chat. and the courage to do things, and the regrets in life, and what I care about, and if there's anything that makes me feel very sparkly. When I look back, I think that might be the most content-driven conversation I've had in a long time. It didn't give me any information. I think I have a more human side to me.

So I've actually made a clear line in my mind. If I'm going to teach AI about information, knowledge, and analysis, but seeing people might become simpler. I'll observe others, and talk about my mental state, and talk about something more sincere, even something that we didn't talk about before. Otherwise, it will really waste the time of two people. Then maybe both of them will talk to AI and the effect will be better.

Yes, when you say these sincere things, it will be a little embarrassing. Just like you said you installed OpenCell, but didn't install it, and then it's painful.

I think this is also the original intention of our program. We also hope to form some real, sincere connections with the audience.

So I think the information I mentioned before should be public information. It's more suitable for agents to summarize and use. Maybe the last paragraph will be a little meaningful.

语言与世界

By the way, our studio is called "Language and World Studio". When you first heard the name, what did you think of?

I didn't think much about it before. It's not particularly sensitive.

Now I think it's a little bit like what we just said, which is the meaning of the AI era organizational structure. That is to say, today's society often, I think, language is used to translate information and transmit information. After this, I think AI will do this job. But language will often be used to establish a real connection between people. I think this is something that will become more and more precious.

What is the most important thing in your life?

Language is World. We hope to explore a new world with you.
